title: Gastrochilus bifidus
taxonomy: Gastrochilus bifidus is a species of orchid in the genus Gastrochilus. This genus is part of the subtribe Gastrochilinae, which is within the family Orchidaceae. The subtribe Gastrochilinae is a group of orchids that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Gastrochilus bifidus is native to the Himalayas and is often found growing on trees and rocks. The classification of Gastrochilus bifidus reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other orchid species.

intro: Gastrochilus bifidus, also known as the Indian pipewort, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and parts of India. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus bifidus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gastrochilus bifidus is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of extracts and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
